differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Explicit reactive primitives manage state updates efficiently without a virtual DOM.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles components into highly efficient imperative JavaScript at build time.

differences.title: 2. VIRTUAL DOM
differences.leftDesc: Updates DOM directly for performance. No VDOM overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Components compile to direct DOM manipulations. No VDOM.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Extremely fast runtime performance due to fine-grained reactivity.
differences.rightDesc: Blazing fast initial load and runtime performance.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Requires understanding reactive primitives like signals.
differences.rightDesc: Simpler syntax, easier to grasp for beginners.

differences.title: 5. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Smaller but rapidly expanding community and library support.
differences.rightDesc: Established ecosystem with rich tooling and community.

differences.title: 6. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Built-in reactive signals for effective local and global state.
differences.rightDesc: Provides built-in reactive stores for state management.

differences.title: 7. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Produces very small bundles due to efficient compilation and no VDOM.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles away framework code, resulting in minimal bundles.
